description: Canonical mistake taxonomy. Seeds behavior:18-log-mistake's canonical-or-mint resolution. Grows via cli-tool:propose-mistake after 3+ distinct learners mint the same mistake-id. Mirrors data:analogy-registry and data:concept-registry patterns. Mistakes are language-agnostic CS-fundamentals where possible; language-specific mistakes are tagged with `applies_to`.

mistakes.label: Forgot null/None/undefined check before use
mistakes.tactics_hint: Pattern repeats when learner thinks of happy-path first. Tactic rotation often lands on tiny test that asserts behavior on null input.

mistakes.label: Passed arguments in wrong order
mistakes.tactics_hint: Pattern repeats when function signature has same-type params (two strings, two ints). Tactic rotation often lands on named-args refactor or a one-line checklist of param order.

mistakes.label: Off-by-one error in loop bound or index
mistakes.tactics_hint: Classic. Tiny test on boundary cases (len=0, len=1, len=N) almost always catches it on the next pass.

mistakes.label: Called async function without await
mistakes.tactics_hint: Pattern repeats when async creeps in mid-build. Tactic rotation often lands on a checklist: 'any call returning a Promise/Task -> needs await OR explicit .then'.

mistakes.label: Mutable default argument shared across calls
mistakes.tactics_hint: Python-specific gotcha. Pair-debug from the failure mode usually lands faster than a checklist — the bug is invisible without running it twice.

mistakes.label: Bare except / catch-all swallows real errors
mistakes.tactics_hint: Pattern repeats when learner is trying to make red go green. Tactic rotation often lands on writing a test that asserts the SPECIFIC exception type.

mistakes.label: Compared string to int (e.g. '5' == 5)
mistakes.tactics_hint: Common when input comes from CLI args, env vars, or JSON. Tactic rotation often lands on a one-line checklist at the input boundary.

mistakes.label: Function modifies state but forgot to return result
mistakes.tactics_hint: Pattern repeats when learner thinks imperatively. Tiny test asserting return value usually catches it.

mistakes.label: Used wrong collection length or range bound
mistakes.tactics_hint: Sibling of off-by-one but different root cause. Tactic rotation: pair-debug stepping through the iteration count helps more than a checklist.

mistakes.label: Committed secret (API key, password, connection string) in source
mistakes.tactics_hint: Severity is high — always intervene on first occurrence, not third. Tactic: walk through env-var or secret-manager pattern immediately.
